source_rationale: PRG4 (proteoglycan 4/lubricin) is a marker of synovial fibroblasts, indicating a fibroblast identity
FBN1 (fibrillin-1) is an ECM gene highly expressed in fibroblasts
PI16 is a marker of universal/adventitial fibroblasts in multiple tissues
COL1A1 encodes type I collagen, a hallmark of fibroblast ECM production
CD34 marks a subset of interstitial/adventitial fibroblasts
MFAP5 (microfibril-associated protein 5) is characteristic of matrix-producing fibroblasts
Decorin is a proteoglycan produced by fibroblasts, a canonical fibroblast marker
THY1 (CD90) is a well-known surface marker of fibroblasts
PDGFRL is related to PDGF receptor signaling characteristic of fibroblast lineage
CD248 (endosialin) marks stromal fibroblasts and pericytes
IL1RL1 (ST2) is a marker of Th2 cells and tissue-resident T helper cells
GATA3 is the master transcription factor for Th2 cell differentiation
KLRB1 (CD161) marks Th17/innate-like T cells and is highly expressed here
HPGD is associated with regulatory/Th2 T cells involved in prostaglandin metabolism
IL7R (CD127) is a marker of memory/naive T helper cells
CCR4 is a chemokine receptor characteristic of Th2 and Treg cells
PTGDR2 (CRTH2) is a definitive marker of Th2 cells
HPGDS (hematopoietic prostaglandin D synthase) is characteristic of Th2 cells
IL32 is highly expressed in activated T cells
RORA is a transcription factor associated with Th2/ILC2 lineage

DLL1 (Delta-like 1) is involved in Notch signaling in T cell development
